title: allium sativum var. vulgare
intro: Allium sativum var. vulgare, commonly known as cultivated garlic, is a plant species belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family, prized for its pungent flavor and medicinal properties. The bulb of this plant contains compounds like allicin, which exhibit antibacterial, antiviral, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating a range of health conditions. Cultivated garlic has been associated with various health benefits, including lowering cholesterol levels, reducing blood pressure, and alleviating symptoms of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Allium sativum var. vulgare include fresh garlic cloves, oil extracts, and dried powders, which can be used in cooking, teas, or supplements to harness its medicinal properties.
